summary: Completed comprehensive testing of streaming availability integration (Movies of the Night API). Backend: All 17 tests passed covering streaming endpoint, allowed services filter, country codes, MongoDB caching, error handling, and movie detail integration. Frontend: Where to Watch section displays correctly with service name, type badge (Stream/Rent/Buy), colored dots, clickable links, and prices. 'No streaming options' fallback message works.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/movies/155/streaming?country=us returns streaming options for The Dark Knight (Max, Hulu, Prime)
Backend: GET /api/movies/238/streaming?country=us returns streaming options for The Godfather (Paramount+, Hulu)
Backend: Streaming options have required fields: service_id, service_name, service_color, type, link
Backend: Rent/buy options include price, price_amount, price_currency fields
Backend: Only allowed services returned (netflix, prime, disney, hulu, apple, hbo, paramount)
Backend: Service display names correct (hbo -> Max, prime -> Prime Video)
Backend: Country code US works correctly
Backend: Country code GB works with GBP pricing
Backend: Country code CA works correctly
Backend: Country codes normalized to lowercase
Backend: MongoDB caching works - second call returns cached=true
Backend: Cached results identical to fresh results
Backend: Invalid movie ID returns empty results (not 500 error)
Backend: Default country is 'us' when not specified
Backend: Movie detail endpoint returns all required fields (title, rating, runtime, genres, cast, similar)
Backend: Movie cast array has name and character fields
Backend: Movie similar array has id, title, poster_url fields
Frontend: Where to Watch section visible in MovieDetail modal (data-testid='where-to-watch')
Frontend: Streaming options show service name with colored dot indicator
Frontend: Streaming options show type badge (Stream/Free/Add-on/Rent/Buy)
Frontend: Streaming options are clickable links to streaming service
Frontend: Prices displayed for rent/buy options (e.g., '3.99 USD')
Frontend: 'No streaming options found for your region' displays when no streaming available
Frontend: Clicking similar movies loads streaming for new movie
Frontend: Movie detail modal shows title, rating, runtime, genres, overview correctly
Frontend: Cast section displays correctly
Frontend: Similar Movies section displays and is clickable
critical_code_review_comments: Streaming API integration is robust with proper 24hr MongoDB caching to conserve API calls
SERVICE_DISPLAY mapping correctly converts internal IDs to user-friendly names (hbo -> Max)
ALLOWED_SERVICES filter prevents unwanted services from appearing
getUserCountry() in frontend correctly extracts country from navigator.language
Error handling graceful - invalid TMDB IDs return empty arrays, not 500 errors
seed_data_creation: Used existing movies: The Dark Knight (155), The Godfather (238), Scream VI - all have known streaming availability
context_for_next_testing_agent: Streaming availability integration is complete. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_streaming.py covers all scenarios. API key has 1000 request limit so tests rely on MongoDB caching (collection: streaming_cache). Known test movies: The Dark Knight (155) has Max/Hulu/Prime in US, The Godfather (238) has Paramount+/Hulu in US. Country codes: us, gb, ca all tested. The 'Where to Watch' section uses data-testid='where-to-watch' and individual options use data-testid='streaming-{service_id}'.

streaming_integration_details.api: Movies of the Night (RapidAPI Streaming Availability)
